mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in over 65 percent of periods where UNRATE exceeds 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer and investment demand, producing negative equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary supply shock that forces monetary tightening, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral when both INDPRO and CPIAUCSL rise concurrently over a 24-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect robust economic activity and corporate hiring, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over five trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to produce a positive directional bias in 5-day forward returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 0.3 percent trigger expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally consistently following CPIAUCSL monthly prints exceeding 0.3 percent, invalidating the tightening fear channel.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales reveals underlying demand fragility that eventually drags corporate earnings and equity prices lower over a quarter.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric yields a Rank IC below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows, showing no predictive power for 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 115 index points signals severe manufacturing contraction that triggers short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over five days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 70 percent of observations when INDPRO drops below the 115 threshold.
